title: 第 155 節
en: la. 'Yath& ah& vairjd: The will of the Lord is the law of holiness ....
'I bless the sacrifice and prajer, and the strength and vigour of Hanrvatfl/, the master ; of the prosperity of the seasons and of the years, the masters of holiness.
'Asliem Vohft : Holiness is the best of all good ....
'[Give] unto that man^ brightness and glory, .... give him the bright, all happy, blissful abode of the holy Ones.'
V. AbAn yast.

The Ab&n Yait (or Yait of the Waters) is devoted to the great goddess of the waters, the celebrated Ardvi Sftra Anfthita, the 'AraiTic of the Greeks. Ardvi Siira An&hita (' the high, powerful, undefiled ') is the heavenly spring from which all waters on the earth flow down ; her fountains are on the top of the mythical mountain, the Hukairya, in the star region. Her descent from the heavens is described in $§ 85 seq.; it reminds one of the Indian legend of the celestial Gangft.

This Yajt contains much valuable information about the historical legends of Iran, as it enumerates the several heroes who worshipped Ardvi Sftra and asked for her help. First of all is Ahura himself (§ 16) ; then came Haoshyangha (§ 21), Yima (§ 25), Asi Dah&ka (§ 29), Thra^taona (§ 33), Keresispa (§ 37), Franghrasyan (§ 41), Kava Usa {§ 45), Husravah (§ 49), Tusa (J 63). Vafisaka's sons (§ 57), Vafra Nav&za (§ 6x), Cr&ro&spa (§ 68), Ashavazdah, the son of PourudhAkhxti, and Ashavazdah and Thrita, the sons of Siyusdri {§ 72), Vistauru (§ 76), Yflirta (§81); the Hvdvas and the Naotaras (§ 98), Zarathunra (§ 103), Kava Vfjtftspa (§ 107), Zairivairi (§ 112), Arc^-aspa and Vandaremaini This enumeration is interrupted by a description of the descent of Ardvi Sftra from the heavens (§§ 85-89), and of certain rules for her sacrifice given by herself to Zaralhujtra (§§ 90-97). This interruption may have been intentional, as it takes place just when Who shall have sacrificed to Haurvat^.
AbAn yast. 53 the course of the enumeration brings us to the times of Zarathuxtra and of the institution of the new religion.
The Yart is opened with a laudation of the benefits bestowed bj Ardvi Siira ($§ 1-16), and it closes with a description of her garments and apparel.

The first record of the worship of Ardvi Siira is in a cuneiform inscription by Artaxerxes Mnemon (404-361), in which her name is corrupted into Anahata. Artaxerxes Mnemon appears to have been an eager promoter of her worship, as he is said 'to have first erected the statues of Venus-An&hita CA^podinyr, 'Ayamdos) in Babylon, Suza, and Ecbatana, and to have taught her worship to the Persians, the Bactrians, and the people of Damas and Sardes ' (Clemens Alexandrinus, Protrept. 5, on the authority of Berosus ; about 360 B.C.). My friend M. Hal^vy suggests to me that the detailed and circumstantial description of Anihiia's appearance and costume (in §§ 1 26-1 31) shows that the writer must have described her from a consecrated type of statuary.

The principal data of the Greek writers on Anfthita will be found in Windischmann's Essay (Die persische Anahita oder Anaftis, 1856). One must be cautious in the use of the Greek sources, as the Greeks, with the eclectic turn of their mind, were inclined to confound under the name of An&hita all the great female deities of Asia Minor, and her name became a common appellation for the Aphrodites as well as for the Artemides of the East o. May Ahura Mazda be rejoiced I . . . .
Ashem Vohii : Holiness is the best of all good ....
I confess myself a worshipper of Mazda, a follower of Zarathartra, one who hates the Dafivas and obeys the laws of Ahura ;
For sacrifice, prayer, propitiation, and glorification unto [H&vani^ the holy and master of holiness ....

Unto the good Waters, made by Mazda ; unto the holy water-spring Ardvi AnAhita ; unto all waters, made by Mazda ; unto all plants, made by Mazda ^, Be propitiation, with sacrifice, prayer, propitiation, and glorification* Yathi ahii vairy6: The will of the Lord is the law of holiness ....
^ Sirdzah I, 10.
54 YAiffTS AND stRdZAHS, I.

K Ahura Mazda spake unto Spitama Zarathurtra, saying i ' Offer up a sacrifice, O Spitama Zarathurtra I unto this spring of mine, Ardvi SClra AnAhita, the wide-expanding^ and health-giving, who hates the Da^vas and obeys the laws of Ahura, who is worthy of sacrifice irt the material world, worthy of prayer in the material world; the life-increasing' and holy, the herd.increasing and holy, the fold-increasing and holy, the wealth-increasing and holy, the countryincreasing and holy;

2. 'Who makes the seed of all males pure^ who makes the womb of all females, pure for bringing forth *, who makes all females bring forth in safety, who puts milk into the breasts of all females in the right measure and the right quality ;
3. * The large river, known afar, that is as large as the whole of the waters that run along the earth ; that runs powerfully from the height Hukair}'a* down to the sea Vouru- Kasha*.
4. ' All the shores of the sea Vouru-Kasha are ^ 'As she comes down to aill places (Phi. tr. ad Yasna LXY, i [LXVI, ,]).
' Adhu, translated ^in ; 'she makes life longer' (Aspendi4rji). Perhaps ddhu will be better translated springs, risers (reading gUy instead of ^4n ; cf. Yt. VIII, 29).
' * Pure and sound, without blood and filth ' (Phi. tr.).

" ' H(igar the lofty is that from which the water of Arfidvtvsftr leaps down the height of a thousand men ' (Bundahii XII, 5, tr. West); cf. infra, §§ 96, lai, 126; Yt. XIII, 24. The Hukairya is mentioned again § 25 andYt. IX, 8; Yt. X, 88; Yt. XV, 15, Yt. XVII, 28. It appears to be situated in the west (Bundahix XXIV, 17; II, 7; Minokhired XLIV, 12).

Abak ya^t. 55 boiling over, all the middle of it is boiling over, when she runs down there, when she streams down there, she, Ardvi SAra An4hita, who has a thousand cells and a thousand channels^: the extent of each of those cells, of each of those channels is as much as a man can ride in forty days, riding on a good horse.

5. ' From this river of mine alone flow all the waters that spread all over the seven Karshvares ; this river of mine alone goes on bringing waters, both in summer and in winter. This river of mine purifies the seed in males, the womb in females, the milk in females' breasts.

6. * I, Ahura Mazda, brought it dow^n with mighty vigour, for the increase of the house, of the borough, of the town, of the country, to keep them, to maintain them, to look over them, to keep and maintain them close.

7. ' Then Ardvi SAra Anihita, O Spitama 7arathuJtra! proceeded forth from the Maker Mazda. Beautiful were her white arms, thick as a horse s shoulder or still thicker ; beautiful was her . . . . *, and thus came she, strong, with thick arms, thinkinpf thus in her heart :

8. * " Who will praise me ? Who will offer me a sacrifice, with libations cleanly prepared and wellstrained, together with the Haoma and meat ? To whom shall I cleave, who cleaves unto me, and thinks with me, and bestows gifts upon me, and is of good will unto me ? ■ "
zh: 一‧耶斯那經文（Yathā ahū vairyō）：主之意志乃聖潔之法……
我祝頌祭祀與禱告，以及哈姆瓦塔（Hanvratāṭ）的力量與活力，他是主人；祝頌季節與年歲的繁榮，他們是聖性的主人。
阿舍姆‧沃胡（Asliem Vohft）：聖潔是一切美善中最殊勝者……
「[賜予]那個人光輝與榮耀……賜予他光明、圓滿幸福、諸聖者的極樂淨土。」
(Aban Yast)

《阿布恩‧雅依特》（或稱《水之雅依特》Abān Yait）乃獻給偉大的水之女神、赫赫有名的阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿那希塔（Ardvi Sūra Anāhita），即希臘人所稱的阿拉克西塔（'Araxic'）。阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿那希塔（Ardvi Sūra Anāhita，意為「高貴的、強大的、不污穢的」）乃天界之泉源，地上萬水皆由此流注而下；其泉眼位於神話中之山峰胡卡依里亞（Hukairya）的山頂，在星宿之域。第§§ 85 等節描述她從天界的降臨；此景象令人想起印度傳說中天上的恆河（Gangā）。

本亞斯特（Yajt）包含許多關於伊朗歷史傳說的寶貴信息，因為它列舉了多位向阿爾德維‧蘇拉（Ardvi Sftra）祈禱並尋求其幫助的英雄。首先是阿胡拉本身（§ 16）；其次是哈歐什揚加（Haoshyangha，§ 21）、伊瑪（Yima，§ 25）、阿西‧達赫卡（Asi Dah&ka，§ 29）、特拉塔歐那（Thra^taona，§ 33）、刻雷西斯帕（Keresispa，§ 37）、弗朗赫拉斯揚（Franghrasyan，§ 41）、卡瓦‧烏薩（Kava Usa，§ 45）、胡斯拉瓦（Husravah，§ 49）、圖薩（Tusa，§ 63）、瓦菲薩卡之子（§ 57）、瓦弗拉‧那瓦扎（Vafra Nav&za，§ 61）、克拉歐斯帕（Cr&ro&spa，§ 68）、阿沙瓦茲達（Ashavazdah，普魯達赫什蒂（PourudhAkhxti）之子）以及阿沙瓦茲達和特里塔（Thrita，西尤斯德里（Siyusdri）之子，§ 72）、維斯陶魯（Vistauru，§ 76）、伊菲爾塔（Yflirta，§ 81）；胡德瓦斯人與納歐塔拉斯人（§ 98）、查拉圖斯特拉（Zarathunra，§ 103）、卡瓦‧維什塔斯帕（Kava Vfjtftspa，§ 107）、扎伊里瓦伊里（Zairivairi，§ 112）、阿雷卡斯帕與萬達雷邁尼。此列舉為對阿爾德維‧蘇拉從天界下降之描述所中斷（§§ 85-89），以及查拉圖斯特拉自己所傳授之其祭祀的某些規則（§§ 90-97）。此中斷可能是有意為之，因為它發生在「誰將向豪爾瓦塔祭祀」之處。
阿邦雅斯特（Aban yast）。53 列舉的進程將我們帶到瑣羅亞斯德的時代，以及新宗教制度的建立。
《亞斯特》(Yart)以頌讚阿爾德維‧蘇拉(Ardvi Sūra)所賜予之恩澤而開篇(§ 1-16)，並以描述她的衣著與裝飾而結尾。

阿爾德維‧西拉（Ardvi Siira）的祭祀最早記載於阿爾塔薛西斯‧姆涅蒙（Artaxerxes Mnemon，公元前404-361年）的楔形文字銘文中，其中她的名字被訛作阿納哈塔（Anahata）。阿爾塔薛西斯‧姆涅蒙似乎是她祭祀的熱心推動者，據說他「首先在巴比倫、蘇薩和埃克巴坦那豎立了金星‧安希塔（Venus-Anahita）的雕像，並向波斯人、巴克特里亞人及大馬士革和薩爾迪斯的民眾傳播她的祭祀」（克萊門斯‧亞歷山德里亞，《勸誡篇》第5卷，引自貝羅索斯之記載；約公元前360年）。我的朋友M‧哈勒維向我指出，阿尼希塔的外貌和服飾的詳細而具體的描述（見§§ 1 26-1 31）表明著述者必然是根據某一種經過祝聖的雕像典範來描寫她的。
關於古代希臘作家論述阿納希塔（Anfthita）的主要資料，可見於溫迪施曼的論文（《波斯的阿納希塔或阿納夫蒂斯》，一八五六年）。必須謹慎使用希臘文獻，因為希臘人以其兼容並蓄的心態，傾向於將亞細亞小亞細亞的所有偉大女神混為一談，並以阿納希塔之名稱之，其名遂成為東方阿佛洛狄特（Aphrodites）以及阿爾忒彌斯（Artemides）的通稱。願阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）欣悅焉……
阿舍姆‧沃希（Ashem Vohii）：聖潔是一切美善中最殊勝者……
我自誠為阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）的信奉者，查拉圖斯特拉（Zarathustra）的追隨者，仇恨諸魔神（Daivas）者，遵奉阿胡拉（Ahura）之律法者；
獻祭、祈禱、懺悔與讚頌，獻予[哈瓦尼（Hāvani）]聖潔者、聖潔之主……
對善良的水獻祭，乃馬茲達所造；對聖潔的水泉阿爾德維‧阿納希塔獻祭；對所有水獻祭，乃馬茲達所造；對所有植物獻祭，乃馬茲達所造，願以和解、祭祀、祈禱、和解與讚頌而獻祭。 雅蒂‧阿希‧瓦伊瑞約（Yathi ahii vairy6）：主的意志乃聖潔之律……
^ 《西爾德札經》(Sirdzah) 第一卷，第十章。
54 雅夫納斯與斯圖達斯，第一卷
阿胡拉‧瑪茲達對斯皮塔瑪‧查拉圖斯特拉說道：「斯皮塔瑪‧查拉圖斯特拉啊，你應當向我的這道泉水——寬廣展開的阿爾德維‧蘇拉‧阿納希塔獻祭。她能增進健康，憎恨達埃瓦族，遵守阿胡拉‧瑪茲達的法則，在物質世界中堪受祭祀，在物質世界中堪受祈禱；她乃生命增長而聖潔者，牲畜增長而聖潔者，畜舍增長而聖潔者，財富增長而聖潔者，邦國增長而聖潔者。
2. 誰使一切男性的種子純淨^，誰使一切女性的子宮純淨以便生育*，誰使一切女性安全地生育，誰以適當的份量和適當的品質將乳汁注入一切女性的乳房；
3. * 那條遠近聞名的大河，其規模如同流經大地的所有水流之總和；它自崇峻的胡凱里亞（Hukair}'a）高地強勢奔流而下，直至沃魯卡沙海（Vouru-Kasha）。
4. 「沃魯-卡沙（Vouru-Kasha）大海的所有海岸都是^ 『當她降臨到所有的地方時』（參閱 Phi. tr. ad Yasna LXV, i [LXVI, ]）。」
'Adhu'，翻譯作「她延長生命」(Aspendi4rji)。也許 adhu 更恰當的譯法是泉源、上升者（讀作 gUy 而非 ^4n；參看 Yt. VIII, 29）。
‧ 「純淨而健全，無血汙與穢垢」（Phi. 譯）。

'希迦爾（高聳之處）乃是阿爾菲德瓦茲斯特河（Arfidvtvsftr）之水從千人高度奔流而下之所'（《邦達希什》XII, 5，韋斯特譯）；參見下文第 96、101、126 節；《雅什特》XIII, 24。胡開爾雅（Hukairya）復見於第 25 節及《雅什特》IX, 8；X, 88；XV, 15；XVII, 28。其位置似乎在西方（《邦達希什》XXIV, 17；II, 7；《米諾赫瑞德》XLIV, 12）。
正文與註釋。
阿巴克亞特。55 沸騰奔湧，它的全部中間都在沸騰奔湧，當她奔流而下時，當她傾瀉而下時，她——阿爾德維‧薩拉‧阿納希塔（Ardvi Sâra Anâhita），擁有千個水室與千條水渠者——那些水室中每一個、那些水渠中每一個的廣度，都相當於一個人騎著良馬能在四十日內馳騁的距離。
5. 「唯獨從我此河流出的水，遍布於七個卡爾什瓦雷（Karshvares）全地；唯獨我此河流，無論夏冬，皆恆運水流。我此河流淨化男子之種，淨化女子之子宮，淨化女子乳房中之乳汁。」
6. * 我，阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda），以強大的威力將其頒布下來，為了家戶、鄰里、城鎮、國土的昌盛，以保護它們、維持它們、監督它們，並將它們緊密地保守維持。
7. 爾時，阿爾德維‧薩拉‧阿尼希塔（Ardvi Sâra Anâhita），哦，斯皮塔瑪‧查拉圖什特拉！從造物主馬茲達而出。其白皙之臂美妙可觀，粗壯如馬之肩膀，或更加粗壯；其……*美妙可觀，如是強大而臂粗之她前來，心中思想如此：
8. * 「誰將讚頌我？誰將向我獻祭，以清潔妥善調製與充分過濾的奠酒、搭配俶摩（Haoma）與祭肉？我應當親近誰？誰親近我，與我同思，向我賜予禮物，對我懷抱善意？」■
